raw_output:
Full review comments:

- [P2] Treat only an enabled CI flag as CI — /workspaces/sleep-optimizer/.claude/worktrees/agent-a3d13ccdbf912cf26/Makefile:29-29
  When a local environment exports `CI=false`, GNU Make's `ifdef CI` still selects this branch, so setup uses the system interpreter and `--system` instead of creating `.venv`. On a PEP 668-managed macOS/Linux Python this reproduces the installation failure this patch is intended to fix; test the flag's value or use the GitHub-specific environment marker instead.

- [P2] Keep the standalone DB-location target runnable — /workspaces/sleep-optimizer/.claude/worktrees/agent-a3d13ccdbf912cf26/Makefile:69-70
  When no virtualenv is active and `.venv` has not been created, the documented standalone `make db-location ARGS="--path …"` command now immediately fails because `.venv/bin/python` does not exist. This affects clean checkouts and users upgrading from the previous system-install workflow; either make this target ensure the selected environment exists or retain a usable interpreter fallback.

findings.summary: ifdef CI treats CI=false as CI — local shells exporting CI=false get --system installs
findings.reason: Value-tested: ifeq ($(CI),true). Verified CI=false now creates the venv.

findings.summary: Standalone make db-location fails on a clean checkout before the venv exists
findings.reason: Env creation extracted to ensure-python, shared prerequisite of setup-backend and db-location; CI argv unchanged.
